questions.question: In a right triangle, the side opposite angle θ has length 5 and the hypotenuse has length 13. Find sin(θ).
A ladder leans against a wall at an angle of 62° to the ground. If the ladder is 10 feet long, how far from the wall is the base of the ladder? Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: The base distance is adjacent to the 62° angle. Use cos(62°) = adjacent/10, so distance = 10·cos(62°) ≈ 10(0.469) ≈ 4.7 feet.

questions.question: If cos(θ) = 3/5 and θ is in the first quadrant, find tan(θ).
questions.explanation: From cos(θ) = 3/5, use sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1 to get sin(θ) = 4/5. Then tan(θ) = sin(θ)/cos(θ) = (4/5)/(3/5) = 4/3.

questions.question: On the unit circle, a point is located at angle 240° from the positive x-axis. What are its coordinates?
questions.explanation: 240° = 180° + 60°, placing the point in the third quadrant. The reference angle is 60°, so coordinates are (-cos(60°), -sin(60°)) = (-1/2, -√3/2).

questions.question: Simplify: sin²(θ) + sin²(θ)·tan²(θ).
questions.explanation: Factor: sin²(θ)(1 + tan²(θ)). Using the identity 1 + tan²(θ) = sec²(θ) = 1/cos²(θ), we get sin²(θ)/cos²(θ) = tan²(θ).

questions.question: A surveyor measures that the angle of elevation to the top of a building is 35° from a point 50 meters away on level ground. Find the height of the building to the nearest meter.
questions.explanation: The horizontal distance is adjacent to the 35° angle. Use tan(35°) = height/50, so height = 50·tan(35°) ≈ 50(0.700) ≈ 35 meters.
